UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile, storing information like their name, email, college, degree, summary, projects, hobbies, goals, skills, and profile image.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

summary.description: A brief summary or bio of the user.

projects.description: A list or markdown-style representation of the user's projects.

skills.description: A list of the user's skills.

profileImageURL.description: URL of the user's profile image.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents an uploaded document, along with its summary, highlighted topics, generated flashcards, and flowchart (if available).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

filename.description: The original filename of the uploaded document.

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the document was uploaded.

summary.description: A summary of the document content generated by Gemini.

highlightedTopics.description: Highlighted important topics from the document, generated by Gemini.

flowchartURL.description: URL of the flowchart generated from the document (if available).

Flashcard.title: Flashcard
Flashcard.description: Represents a flashcard generated from a document, consisting of a question and an answer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Flashcard entity.

answer.description: The answer to the question on the flashcard.

PyqAnswer.title: PyqAnswer
PyqAnswer.description: Represents an answer generated for a past year question (PYQ).

id.description: Unique identifier for the PyqAnswer entity.

answer.description: The generated answer for the question.

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the PYQ answer was generated.

Timetable.title: Timetable
Timetable.description: Represents a study timetable generated for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Timetable entity.

subjectHours.description: JSON string representing an array of subject and study hours (e.g., `[{subject: 'Math', hours: 2}, {subject: 'History', hours: 1}]`)

generatedDate.description: The date and time when the timetable was generated.

TodoTask.title: TodoTask
TodoTask.description: Represents a task in the to-do list or assignment tracker.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TodoTask entity.

completed.description: Indicates whether the task is completed.

VoiceNote.title: VoiceNote
VoiceNote.description: Represents a voice-to-text note taken by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VoiceNote entity.

text.description: The text content of the voice note.

creationDate.description: The date and time when the voice note was created.

GameScore.title: GameScore
GameScore.description: Represents the score achieved by a user in a specific game.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameScore entity.

gameName.description: The name of the game (e.g., Puzzle, Memory, Quiz).

score.description: The score achieved by the user in the game.

playDate.description: The date and time when the game was played.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Path-based ownership: Only the user with the matching {userId} can access this document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores documents uploaded by the user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who owns the document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document.

definition.description: Stores flashcards associated with a document. Includes denormalized 'userId' from the parent document for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who owns the flashcard.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document associated with the flashcard.

params.name: flashcardId
params.description: The unique identifier for the flashcard.

definition.description: Stores answers to past year questions generated by the user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who created the answer.

params.name: pyqAnswerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the PYQ answer.

definition.description: Stores timetables generated by the user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who owns the timetable.

params.name: timetableId
params.description: The unique identifier for the timetable.

definition.description: Stores to-do tasks created by the user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who owns the to-do task.

params.name: todoTaskId
params.description: The unique identifier for the to-do task.

definition.description: Stores voice notes created by the user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who owns the voice note.

params.name: voiceNoteId
params.description: The unique identifier for the voice note.

definition.description: Stores game scores achieved by the user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who achieved the game score.

params.name: gameScoreId
params.description: The unique identifier for the game score.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to provide a secure, scalable, and easily maintainable backend for the Noether application, prioritizing authorization independence and clear access patterns. User-specific data is stored under `/users/{userId}` to ensure only the authenticated user can access it. Denormalization is used to avoid hierarchical dependencies and enable atomic operations. For example, each document and other entities store the userId for simple ownership checks in security rules. Segregation is used by storing user profiles separately from other collections, ensuring consistent security requirements for documents within those collections.

**Authorization Independence:** Each document related to a user includes the `userId` field, enabling direct authorization checks without needing to retrieve parent document data. For example, Documents, Flashcards, PyqAnswers, Timetables, TodoTasks, VoiceNotes, and GameScores all include the `userId` field. This allows security rules to validate `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without `get()` calls.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** User-specific data is stored under path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`), enabling secure `list` operations. Rules can efficiently filter collections based on the `userId` without needing to read the entire collection. Furthermore, separating data with different access requirements (e.g., user profiles vs. shared content - if implemented later) ensures that rules are not used as filters, maintaining security and performance.
